title: Ultrasonic Piezoelectric Atomiser (Humidifier)
content: An ultrasonic atomiser uses a piezoelectric disc vibrating at 1–3 MHz to generate standing waves on the water surface, ejecting micro-droplets (1–5 µm) as a cool mist without heating.
extended_content: Drop diameter: D ≈ (8π·σ / (ρ·f²))^(1/3) (Lang equation), where σ = surface tension, ρ = density, f = frequency.  At 1.7 MHz: D ≈ 3–5 µm (respirable range — appropriate for humidification; verify mineral deposit accumulation at higher hardness).  Efficiency: 40–100 mL/h at 3–5 W; far more efficient than resistive-heating humidifiers.  Driver circuit: half-bridge with inductance matching to piezo impedance peak at resonance frequency.  RH control: humidity sensor (SHT41) + PI controller adjusts PWM duty cycle of atomiser.  Hard water deposits (scale) on transducer reduce efficiency — use demineralised water or periodic citric acid cleaning.  Silent operation: 20–30 dB(A) vs 50+ dB(A) for fan humidifiers.
units: D: µm, σ: N/m, ρ: kg/m³, f: Hz
source: Lang, Ultrasonic Atomisation of Liquid, J. Acoust. Soc. Am., 1962; Ultrasonic Transducer Application Guide, Murata
